patches.summary: Adapt the published Namecheap CLI to the Namecheap XML API query-parameter authentication model.
patches.reason: Namecheap requires ApiUser, ApiKey, UserName, and ClientIp query parameters instead of bearer headers; the published CLI must inject and advertise the credentials users actually configure.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... and focused auth/cache regression tests pass.

patches.summary: Sort GET query parameters before hashing cache keys.
patches.reason: Go map iteration order is randomized, so cache reads and writes for the same Namecheap request could otherwise hash to different filenames.
patches.validated_outcome: TestCacheKeySortsParameters verifies equivalent parameter maps produce the same cache key.

patches.summary: Replace stale NAMECHEAP_API_KEY_QUERY user-facing references with NAMECHEAP_API_KEY.
patches.reason: config.Load reads NAMECHEAP_API_KEY, so hints and agent/MCP metadata must not direct users to an ignored environment variable.
patches.validated_outcome: TestAuthStatusHintUsesNamecheapAPIKeyEnv verifies auth status hints use NAMECHEAP_API_KEY and omit NAMECHEAP_API_KEY_QUERY.

patches.summary: Mask Namecheap ApiKey query parameters in dry-run output.
patches.reason: Namecheap credentials are injected into query params, so generic bearer-header masking did not protect --dry-run previews.
patches.validated_outcome: TestDryRunMasksNamecheapAPIKeyParam verifies raw API keys are absent and masked values are present.

patches.summary: Persist auth set-token values to the Namecheap api_key config field and clear query-auth credentials on logout.
patches.reason: The generated OAuth-style access_token field is not used by Namecheap request preparation; persisted keys must land in api_key.
patches.validated_outcome: TestAuthSetTokenPersistsNamecheapAPIKey verifies auth set-token writes api_key and leaves access_token empty.

patches.summary: Cache the auto-detected public ClientIp once per Client instance.
patches.reason: Repeated calls to api.ipify.org add latency and make commands fragile when NAMECHEAP_CLIENT_IP is not explicitly configured.
patches.validated_outcome: TestPrepareNamecheapRequestCachesDetectedClientIP verifies two prepared requests perform one ipify lookup.

patches.summary: Treat Namecheap XML ApiResponse Status=ERROR envelopes as command errors.
patches.reason: Namecheap returns HTTP 200 for invalid credentials and other API failures, so the converted JSON body must be inspected before reporting success.

patches.summary: Mark Namecheap paid and DNS-changing MCP tools as destructive and non-read-only.
patches.reason: MCP hosts use read-only/destructive annotations for auto-approval decisions; DNS changes and domain registration/renewal/lock changes must require confirmation.
patches.validated_outcome: Focused source scan and go test ./... verify mutation tools use ReadOnly=false and Destructive=true.

patches.summary: Open MCP SQL stores with SQLite mode=ro for database-enforced read-only protection.
patches.reason: The MCP SQL validator allows WITH queries and relies on the read-only store as a second safety layer; the store must reject writes at the database level.
patches.validated_outcome: TestOpenReadOnlyRejectsWrites verifies writes fail through OpenReadOnly while reads continue to work.

patches.summary: Allowlist and quote generated SQL identifiers in exported store helper functions.
patches.reason: ListIDs and ResolveByName interpolate table names and JSON-path keys because SQLite cannot bind identifiers; generated helper inputs must be identifier-shaped before interpolation.
patches.validated_outcome: TestListIDsRejectsUnsafeResourceTableIdentifier and TestResolveByNameRejectsUnsafeMatchField verify unsafe strings are not interpolated.

patches.summary: Use the full SHA-256 digest for cache filenames.
patches.reason: The generated cache key truncated to 64 bits; keeping the full digest avoids avoidable filename collisions.
patches.validated_outcome: TestCacheKeyUsesFullSHA256Digest verifies cache keys are 64 hex characters.

patches.summary: Return MCP SQL row, column, and JSON encoding errors instead of silently emitting partial results.
patches.reason: The MCP SQL tool should not hide scan or iteration failures when agents use it for local analysis.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/mcp and go test ./... pass.

patches.summary: Use the full SHA-256 digest in the auxiliary internal/cache package.
patches.reason: The auxiliary cache helper should not retain the generated 64-bit truncated filename pattern after the client cache was hardened.
patches.validated_outcome: TestPathUsesFullSHA256Digest verifies the cache filename uses a 64-character digest.

patches.summary: Sanitize MCP transport errors before returning them to hosts.
patches.reason: Go transport errors can include full request URLs; Namecheap authenticates through query params, so MCP error responses must redact ApiKey-style query parameters on every path.
patches.validated_outcome: TestAuthErrorHelpers verifies ApiKey query params are redacted; go test ./... passes.

patches.summary: Write Namecheap client cache directories and files with owner-only permissions.
patches.reason: Cached Namecheap responses can include account, domain, balance, and certificate details; local users should not be able to read them through world-readable cache paths.
patches.validated_outcome: TestWriteCacheUsesPrivatePermissions verifies cache directories use 0700 and cache files use 0600.

patches.summary: Sanitize CLI transport/API error paths before printing to stderr.
patches.reason: Go transport errors can include full request URLs; Namecheap authenticates through query params, so CLI stderr must redact ApiKey-style query parameters like the MCP path.
patches.validated_outcome: TestClassifyAPIErrorSanitizesTransportURLCredentials verifies CLI transport errors do not expose ApiKey query params; go test ./... passes.
